title: dodonaea viscosa var. angustifolia
intro: Dodonaea viscosa var. angustifolia, also known as the narrow-leaved hop-bush, is a variety of the Dodonaea viscosa plant, a small shrub native to Australia and Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The narrow-leaved hop-bush has been used to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Dodonaea viscosa var. angustifolia are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal benefits.
